items.title: JSFB delivered mixed Q4 results with strong sequential recovery in PAT (₹139.8 Cr vs ₹9.7 Cr in Q3) and met growth guidance for advances (24.6%) and...
items.description_html:
JSFB delivered mixed Q4 results with strong sequential recovery in PAT (₹139.8 Cr vs ₹9.7 Cr in Q3) and met growth guidance for advances (24.6%) and deposits (22.9%).

However, FY26 PAT declined 34.9% YoY, significantly missing the 0-2% growth guidance. The Retail Banking turnaround (Q4 profit vs Q3 loss) and improving asset quality (GNPA 2.46%) provide near-term catalysts, but elevated cost-to-income (67.75%) and provisions above guidance remain concerns. Management's FY27 guidance of 14-15% ROE appears ambitious given FY26 ROE of 8.1%.

Positive Factors

Advances grew 24.6% YoY to ₹33,827 Cr, exceeding FY26 guidance of ~20%

Deposits grew 22.9% YoY to ₹35,784 Cr, exceeding FY26 guidance of 18-20%

NII grew 26.5% YoY to ₹7,356 Cr, outpacing advances growth

Retail Banking turned around: Q4 profit ₹755.6 Cr vs Q3 loss ₹620.7 Cr

GNPA improved to 2.46% from 2.71% YoY, NNPA to 0.92% from 0.94%

CAR at 19.38% remains well above regulatory minimum

Q4 PAT recovered sequentially to ₹139.8 Cr from ₹9.7 Cr in Q3

Risk Factors

FY26 PAT declined 34.9% YoY to ₹326.4 Cr, significantly missing guidance of 0-2% growth

Q4 PAT at ₹139.8 Cr marginally missed guidance of ₹140-160 Cr

FY26 provisions at ₹839.2 Cr exceeded guidance range, up 17.5% YoY

Cost-to-income ratio elevated at 67.75% for FY26

Operating expenses grew 26.1% YoY vs income growth of 17.0%

FY26 PPOP declined 1.9% YoY to ₹1,165.7 Cr

items.title: Navin Fluorine delivered exceptional FY26 results with 41% revenue growth and 992 bps margin expansion, significantly beating its 28-30% EBITDA margin...
items.description_html:
Navin Fluorine delivered exceptional FY26 results with 41% revenue growth and 992 bps margin expansion, significantly beating its 28-30% EBITDA margin guidance.

The company achieved a 32.64% Operating EBITDA margin, demonstrating superior operating leverage with expenses growing only 24% vs 41% revenue growth. Strong cash generation (1.35x CFO/PAT) and deleveraging (Debt/Equity from 0.55x to 0.31x) further reinforce the quality of execution.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 41.05% YoY to ₹3,313.90 crores

Operating EBITDA margin expanded 992 bps YoY to 32.64%

PAT grew 129.92% YoY to ₹663.55 crores

Operating cash flow at ₹893.57 crores with 1.35x cash conversion

Debt to equity ratio improved from 0.55x to 0.31x

Free cash flow turned positive at ₹404.31 crores

Subsidiaries contributed 30.5% to revenue and 26.5% to PAT

Risk Factors

Cost of materials grew 38.9% YoY in Q4 vs revenue growth of 33.8%

Capex at ₹489.26 crores was significantly below guidance of ₹700-1,000 crores

Q4 saw inventory build-up with negative change in inventories of ₹39.43 crores

items.title: Bajaj Finance delivered a solid Q4 FY26 with normalized PAT growth of 23.9% YoY meeting its long-term guidance, driven by strong NII growth (21.2% YoY...
items.description_html:
Bajaj Finance delivered a solid Q4 FY26 with normalized PAT growth of 23.9% YoY meeting its long-term guidance, driven by strong NII growth (21.2% YoY) and better-than-expected cost control. The company beat guidance on customer additions (17.51M vs 14-16M target) and gold loan book (₹17,831Cr vs ₹16,000Cr target), while missing AUM growth target (22.4% vs 24-25% guidance).

Gold Loans emerged as a new growth driver with 114.6% YoY expansion. Asset quality remains healthy with GNPA at 1.01%. Management signaled growth moderation to 17-20% for FY27, suggesting the company is transitioning to a more measured growth phase while maintaining profitability within guidance ranges.

Positive Factors

Normalized PAT grew 23.9% YoY to ₹20,689 crore, meeting the long-term guidance of 23-24%

Customer additions of 17.51 million exceeded guidance of 14-16 million

Gold Loan book grew 114.6% YoY to ₹17,831 crore, beating target of ~₹16,000 crore

PBT grew 31.2% YoY outpacing revenue growth of 18.0% due to cost control and lower provisions

Consolidated asset quality stable with GNPA at 1.01% and NNPA at 0.41%

Fee and commission income grew 46.5% YoY in Q4, showing strong non-interest income diversification

Capital adequacy strong at 21.55% CAR with Tier-1 at 20.67%

ROA of 4.6% and ROE of 19.2% both met upper end of guidance ranges

Risk Factors

AUM growth of 22.4% missed guidance of 24-25%, indicating deceleration momentum

MSME Lending AUM grew only 5.9% YoY, reflecting sector stress

Provisions for FY26 up 33.8% YoY to ₹9,482 crore, credit cost at higher end of guidance

Captive 2W & 3W Finance book declined 59.9% YoY as part of strategic run-down

Management guided for moderation in growth to 17-20% for FY27 onwards

Credit cost guidance of 1.85-1.95% was met at upper end (1.93%)

items.title: Schaeffler India delivered strong Q1 CY26 results with 18.9% YoY revenue growth and 25.6% PAT growth. The Mobility segment was the key driver with 39....
items.description_html:
Schaeffler India delivered strong Q1 CY26 results with 18.9% YoY revenue growth and 25.6% PAT growth. The Mobility segment was the key driver with 39.3% EBIT growth and 142 bps margin expansion, while Automotive Technologies grew 30.8% YoY.

However, the subsidiary Koovers reported a material loss of ₹148 Cr, creating a drag on consolidated profitability. Export growth of 32.6% YoY exceeded management's guidance for moderation to 5-10%, but raw material cost pressures (25.6% YoY) persist.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 18.9% YoY to ₹2,585.6 Cr

PAT grew 25.6% YoY to ₹316.1 Cr

EBITDA margin expanded 43 bps to 18.5%

Mobility segment EBIT grew 39.3% YoY with 142 bps margin expansion

Automotive Technologies sub-segment grew 30.8% YoY

Intercompany Exports grew 32.6% YoY

Finance costs declined 24.4% YoY indicating low debt

Risk Factors

Subsidiary KRSV reported net loss of ₹148 Cr on ₹787 Cr revenue, material drag on consolidated profitability

Cost of materials consumed grew 25.6% YoY, faster than revenue

Purchases of stock-in-trade grew 38.8% YoY, indicating mix shift to lower-margin traded goods

Bearings & Industrial Solutions revenue declined 7.3% YoY in Others segment

Inventory build-up of ₹1,374.1 million compared to inventory drawdown prior year

items.title: MASFIN delivered strong operational performance with 25% revenue growth and 32% PPOP growth, demonstrating improved operating efficiency (C/I ratio do...
items.description_html:
MASFIN delivered strong operational performance with 25% revenue growth and 32% PPOP growth, demonstrating improved operating efficiency (C/I ratio down 77bps).

The company met ROA guidance but missed AUM growth and ROE targets. Asset quality showed slight deterioration with 40% rise in provisions. Capital adequacy remains strong at 22.84%. Growth outlook maintained at 20-25% with focus on risk management and profitability.

Positive Factors

Total income grew 25.13% YoY to ₹2,002.27 Crores

PPOP grew 32.2% YoY to ₹822.83 Crores with strong operating leverage

NII grew 24.2% YoY to ₹742.03 Crores, outpacing finance cost growth of 18.9%

Cost-to-Income ratio improved by 77 bps to 24.89%

Normalized PAT grew 20.8% YoY to ₹379.41 Crores (adjusted for one-time charge)

ROA of 2.92% met management's guidance range of 2.75%-3.00%

Capital Adequacy Ratio at 22.84% provides strong buffer for growth

Insurance Broking segment turned profitable (PBT ₹1.04 Cr vs loss ₹0.24 Cr)

Housing Finance subsidiary AUM grew 22.4% YoY with PAT up 40.4% in Q4

Risk Factors

AUM growth of 18.7%/19.0% missed management's guidance range of 20-25%

ROE of 13.43% significantly below target range of 16-18%

Impairment charges rose 40% YoY to ₹179.78 Crores, outpacing loan growth

Gross Stage 3 assets deteriorated from 2.44% to 2.57% YoY

Credit cost at 1.28% of AUM is at higher end of guidance range

Fees & commission income declined 19.8% YoY in Q4

items.title: Adani Power reported strong Q4 with 64% PAT growth driven by 492% surge in other income (CERC order) and ultra-low 2.6% tax rate. Core operations show...
items.description_html:
Adani Power reported strong Q4 with 64% PAT growth driven by 492% surge in other income (CERC order) and ultra-low 2.6% tax rate. Core operations showed healthy 9.3% EBITDA growth with 37% margin and 31.8% segment EBIT growth.

PPA coverage improved to 95% reducing merchant exposure risk. However, results include significant one-time items (₹1,007 Cr CERC income, ₹793 Cr deferred tax credit) that inflate profitability. Capex acceleration (75% above guidance) supports FY27 capacity addition target of 2.9 GW.

Positive Factors

Net Profit grew 64.3% YoY to ₹4,271.40 Cr

Continuing EBITDA grew 9.3% YoY to ₹5,572.64 Cr with 37% margin

Segment EBIT grew 31.8% YoY to ₹4,439.59 Cr with 31.2% margin

PPA coverage improved to 95% from 91% in Q3, targeting 100%

Trade receivables declined 9.4% YoY improving working capital

Operating cash flow strong at ₹20,513.65 Cr with 1.58x cash conversion ratio

Risk Factors

Other Income surge (492% YoY) includes one-time items - CERC order ₹1,007 Cr and prior period income ₹728 Cr - may not recur

Finance costs rose 26.5% YoY to ₹967.26 Cr due to higher debt for capex

Capex overspend 75% above guidance (₹23,350 Cr vs ₹13,307 Cr) increases execution risk

Merchant power rates remained subdued in Q4 impacting revenue growth

Debt increased 39.7% YoY to ₹53,555.54 Cr, Net Debt up 44.8% to ₹45,138 Cr

items.title: Ador Welding delivered strong margin expansion (234 bps to 10.4%) and PAT growth (36.5% YoY) driven by cost control and operating leverage, meeting it...
items.description_html:
Ador Welding delivered strong margin expansion (234 bps to 10.4%) and PAT growth (36.5% YoY) driven by cost control and operating leverage, meeting its margin guidance.

However, revenue growth of 1.5% significantly missed the 10-15% guidance, and the Services segment remains a drag with ₹2,306 Lakhs loss. The core Welding segment showed improvement with 160 bps margin expansion, and the company maintains a strong net cash position. Near-term alpha is moderate given mixed execution on growth guidance.

Positive Factors

EBITDA margin expanded 234 bps YoY to 10.4% in FY26

PAT grew 36.5% YoY to ₹8,198 Lakhs (₹82 Crores)

Normalized PAT grew 61.0% YoY indicating strong core performance

Welding segment EBIT margins expanded 160 bps to 15.2%

Operating margin of 10.3% met management's 10% minimum guidance

Company remains net debt-free with ₹9,154 Lakhs cash

Flares segment turned profitable in Q4 (EBIT 545 Lakhs) after full-year losses

Capex reduced 46% to ₹22.2 Crores vs guidance

Risk Factors

Revenue grew only 1.5% YoY, significantly missing 10-15% guidance

Services segment (FPED) posted ₹2,306 Lakhs loss, missing H1 FY26 break-even target

Working capital days worsened as receivables and inventories grew ~20% while revenue grew 1.7%

CFO declined 15.5% YoY to ₹11,586 Lakhs due to working capital pressure

Flares & Process Equipment segment revenue declined 32.4% YoY

Revenue guidance of doubling by 2029 at risk given 1.5% growth vs required ~15% CAGR

items.title: SHREDIGCEM showed a strong sequential recovery in Q4 with revenue up 13.7% QoQ and EBITDA margin expanding nearly 1000 bps to 12.04%. The Hi-Bond dist...
items.description_html:
SHREDIGCEM showed a strong sequential recovery in Q4 with revenue up 13.7% QoQ and EBITDA margin expanding nearly 1000 bps to 12.04%. The Hi-Bond distributorship significantly exceeded expectations with EBITDA of ₹668/ton vs guidance of ₹200-300/ton.

However, the company took on massive debt (₹513 crore) to fund this initiative, and cash flow deteriorated sharply. The FY27 volume target of 30-35 lac tons implies 109-144% growth, which appears highly ambitious.

Positive Factors

Q4 revenue recovered 13.7% QoQ to ₹20,847 lakh with strong pricing improvement of ₹517/ton

Company swung from loss of ₹698 lakh in Q3 to profit of ₹794 lakh in Q4

EBITDA margin expanded 994 bps QoQ to 12.04% driven by higher realizations and cost control

Hi-Bond distributorship generated EBITDA of ₹668/ton, well above guidance of ₹200-300/ton

Power and fuel costs declined 12.4% YoY and freight costs fell 10.9% YoY

Hi-Bond contributed ₹1,536 lakh revenue in just 12 days of operation

Risk Factors

Total debt increased 368% from ₹109.8 crore to ₹513.3 crore to fund Hi-Bond deal

Operating cash flow deeply negative at ₹41,088 lakh due to ₹400 crore security deposit

Finance costs surged 821% YoY to ₹480 lakh, pressuring profitability

FY27 volume target of 30-35 lac tons requires 109-144% YoY growth, highly ambitious

Revenue declined 3.7% YoY and PAT fell 56.7% YoY indicating YoY weakness

items.title: IIFL delivered strong Q4 with 42.6% revenue growth and 148% PAT growth, beating its loan growth guidance of ~20% with actual 28.1% expansion. Asset qu...
items.description_html:
IIFL delivered strong Q4 with 42.6% revenue growth and 148% PAT growth, beating its loan growth guidance of ~20% with actual 28.1% expansion. Asset quality improved significantly with GNPA falling to 1.46% from 2.2%.

However, profitability metrics ROA (2.32%) and ROE (12.61%) missed guidance, and capital adequacy at 17.84% remains below target. The standalone entity's turnaround from loss to ₹1,153 crore profit is a key positive. Execution is good with operating leverage visible but some efficiency targets were missed.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 42.6% YoY to ₹3,699.67 crore in Q4, full year up 30.6% to ₹13,373.83 crore

PAT grew 148% YoY to ₹623.26 crore in Q4; full year PAT up 214% to ₹1,816.70 crore

Loan book grew 28.1% YoY, exceeding management's ~20% growth guidance

Cost-to-Income ratio improved 784 bps YoY to 43.89% in Q4

GNPA improved to 1.46% from 2.2% in FY25; NNPA at 0.73%

PPOP grew 70.6% YoY to ₹1,172.70 crore in Q4

Standalone entity turned from loss of ₹409.57 crore (FY25) to profit of ₹1,153.52 crore (FY26)

Leverage at 4.43x within management's target range of 4.0-4.5x

Risk Factors

ROA of 2.32% missed management's guidance of 2.5-2.8%

ROE of 12.61% missed management's guidance of ~16%

Operating expense ratio at 5.56% of avg loans missed guidance of 4.2-4.3%

Standalone CRAR at 17.84% below management's target of ~20%

Finance costs grew 37.1% YoY, outpacing interest income growth of 23.7%

Subsidiary PAT contribution (30.5%) trails revenue contribution (44.2%), indicating lower profitability in subsidiary businesses

items.title: Jewellery body asks govt to add physical gold under gold monetisation scheme
items.description_html: Jewellery body asks govt to add physical gold under gold monetisation scheme

items.title: CAPITALSFB delivered a solid Q4 with PPOP growth of 27.7% YoY and improved cost-to-income ratio to 59.16%. The bank met its advance growth target of 2...
items.description_html:
CAPITALSFB delivered a solid Q4 with PPOP growth of 27.7% YoY and improved cost-to-income ratio to 59.16%. The bank met its advance growth target of 20%+ and maintained strong capital adequacy at 22.31%. Asset quality showed sequential improvement with GNPA at 2.54%.

However, retail banking profitability declined and provisions surged, limiting PAT growth to 7.4% for FY26. The bank remains on track for Vision 2029 targets but needs acceleration in advance growth to meet the ₹16,000 Cr target.

Positive Factors

PPOP grew 27.7% YoY in Q4 to ₹59.82 Cr, driven by contained operating expenses

Cost-to-Income ratio improved to 59.16% in Q4 from 64.4% in Q3

Capital Adequacy Ratio improved to 22.31% from 21.60% in Q3

Gross NPA ratio improved to 2.54% from 2.68% in Q3; Net NPA to 1.24% from 1.35%

Provision Coverage Ratio improved to 51.9% from 50.46% in Q3

NIM improved sequentially to 4.06% from 4.01% in Q3

Treasury segment profit doubled to 2,499 Lakhs (+106.5% YoY)

Dividend increased 25% to ₹5 per share from ₹4

Risk Factors

Retail Banking segment profit declined 11.2% YoY in Q4 and 6.9% for FY26

Wholesale Banking profit declined 16.7% YoY in Q4 despite 39.8% revenue growth

Provisions surged 213% YoY in Q4 to 579 Lakhs

Operating Cash Flow was negative at (₹385.09 Cr) for FY26 due to rapid loan growth

Net NPA of 1.24% missed management's target of <1% for FY26

CASA ratio declined to 34.7% from 35.9% QoQ indicating funding mix pressure

items.title: Indian Bank delivered a strong Q4 with advances and deposits significantly exceeding guidance (14.67% vs 10-12%, 12.29% vs 8-10%). The key highlight i...
items.description_html:
Indian Bank delivered a strong Q4 with advances and deposits significantly exceeding guidance (14.67% vs 10-12%, 12.29% vs 8-10%). The key highlight is asset quality turning corner with GNPA at 1.98% - first time below 2%. NII grew 11.27% YoY and ROE at 19.82% met guidance.

Corporate Banking segment showed strong revival with 19% profit growth. Concerns include provisions surge (+54% YoY) and Treasury weakness (-13% profit). Overall, execution was good with multiple guidance beats on growth and asset quality metrics.

Positive Factors

Advances grew 14.67% YoY (guidance 10-12%), deposit growth 12.29% YoY (guidance 8-10%)

GNPA ratio improved to 1.98%, meeting sub-2% target - first time below this threshold

NII grew 11.27% YoY to ₹7,109.51 cr, outpacing interest cost growth of 9.55%

Corporate Banking segment profit surged 19% YoY with 157 bps margin expansion

CET1 ratio rebounded to 16.40% from 14.54% in Q3, strong capital position

PPOP grew 5.32% YoY to ₹5,285.73 cr, ROE at 19.82% within 18-20% guidance

Cost to income ratio improved to 44.99% from 45.04% YoY

Net NPA at very low 0.15%, Provision Coverage Ratio strong at 98.28%

Risk Factors

Provisions surged 54.26% YoY to ₹1,225.76 cr, primarily from standard asset provisions including ₹308.40 cr for West Asia Crisis

Treasury segment profit declined 13.15% YoY with margins contracting 594 bps due to rate volatility

PBT declined 3.89% YoY to ₹4,059.97 cr dragged down by sharp provision increase

Other Income declined 8.88% YoY to ₹2,499.68 cr in Q4, may indicate treasury gains or fee income volatility

CET1 ratio of 16.40% missed ambitious 18% target despite strong capital position

Subsidiaries/associates created ₹451.37 cr drag on consolidated PAT due to SGB write-down

items.title: Federal Bank Q4 showed strong operational performance with 22% PAT growth and 55% PPOP surge driven by 33% NII growth and improved operating leverage....
items.description_html:
Federal Bank Q4 showed strong operational performance with 22% PAT growth and 55% PPOP surge driven by 33% NII growth and improved operating leverage. Asset quality reached multi-year lows (GNPA 1.62%, NNPA 0.20%) and Retail Banking profit doubled YoY confirming MFI recovery.

However, full-year PAT grew only 1.6% and normalized growth was modest at 11% after excluding significant one-off items (IT refund, floating provision, tax write-back). Loan growth of 12.6% missed the high-teen target. The bank demonstrated strong cost control and capital position but guidance was largely missed on key metrics.

Positive Factors

Q4 PAT grew 22.21% YoY to ₹1,25,910 Lakhs (₹1,259 Cr)

PPOP surged 55.34% YoY to ₹2,27,641 Lakhs (₹2,276 Cr) with strong operating leverage

Cost to income ratio improved significantly to 47.27% in Q4

Asset quality reached multi-year lows: GNPA 1.62%, NNPA 0.20%

Retail Banking profit doubled YoY (102%) confirming MFI recovery

Digital Banking turned profitable at ₹2,592 Lakhs vs loss previously

Capital adequacy strengthened to 17.25% from 16.40% YoY

NII grew 33.45% YoY to ₹3,17,261 Lakhs driven by NII growth and liability cost management

Risk Factors

Provisions surged 436.6% YoY to ₹74,104 Lakhs including one-off floating provision of ₹45,596 Lakhs

Normalized PAT growth was only 11.06% YoY (vs reported 22.21%) after excluding one-offs

FY26 full-year PAT grew only 1.61% YoY; normalized PAT declined 1.2% YoY

Loan growth of 12.58% missed high-teen target; credit cost of 54.65 bps slightly above revised guidance of 50-53 bps

Treasury segment revenue declined 11.92% YoY and profit declined 42.21% YoY in Q4

items.title: Govt adds higher ethanol blends, biodiesel in draft motor vehicle rules
items.description_html: Govt adds higher ethanol blends, biodiesel in draft motor vehicle rules

items.title: Vedanta delivered a strong Q4 and FY26 with EBITDA of ₹55,976 Crore beating management's $6 billion guidance by 12%, while achieving Net Debt/EBITDA ...
items.description_html:
Vedanta delivered a strong Q4 and FY26 with EBITDA of ₹55,976 Crore beating management's $6 billion guidance by 12%, while achieving Net Debt/EBITDA of 0.93x vs 1x target.

Margin expansion of 310 bps YoY driven by Aluminium (1600 bps) and Zinc India (650 bps) segments, with Power segment turnaround confirming business inflection. Strong cash generation with FCF of ₹18,623 Crore and 1.89x cash conversion ratio supports deleveraging and capital returns.

Positive Factors

FY26 EBITDA of ₹55,976 Crore beat management guidance of >$6 billion by ~12%

Net Debt/EBITDA improved to 0.93x vs target of 1x, achieving deleveraging goal

Revenue grew 15.8% YoY to ₹1,77,194 Crore with Q4 growth at 30.6% YoY

PAT grew 22.2% YoY to ₹25,096 Crore; normalized PAT grew 51.1% YoY

Aluminium EBITDA margin expanded to 45.2% from 29.2% YoY (1600 bps)

Zinc India EBITDA margin expanded to 61.1% from 54.6% YoY (650 bps)

Power segment turnaround: EBITDA grew 365% YoY, margins from 8.6% to 20.9%

Free cash flow strong at ₹18,623 Crore with 1.89x cash conversion ratio

Risk Factors

Zinc International EBITDA declined 73% YoY to ₹110 Crore due to lower zinc prices

Oil & Gas EBITDA declined 12.1% YoY with margins compressing to 41.2% from 45.6%

Cost of materials consumed rose 41.8% YoY in Q4, faster than revenue

Effective tax rate increased to 29.2% from 23.6% YoY due to earnings mix

Net exceptional loss of ₹4,178 Crore booked for FY26 including impairments

items.title: Vakrangeee delivered strong full-year PAT growth (74.3% YoY) and margin expansion in core Kendra segment (250 bps), with robust cash conversion (5.5x)...
items.description_html:
Vakrangeee delivered strong full-year PAT growth (74.3% YoY) and margin expansion in core Kendra segment (250 bps), with robust cash conversion (5.5x). However, Q4 showed sequential deterioration with revenue decline (10.2% YoY) and profit drop (36.2% QoQ).

The ATM subsidiary (Vortex) underperformed significantly, with Q4 revenue falling 41.1% and losses widening, contradicting management's growth narrative. High dependence on other income (96% of Q4 PBT) is a concern. Operational targets for district coverage appear missed.

Positive Factors

Full-year PAT grew 74.3% YoY to ₹1,126.62 Lakh
Vakrangee Kendra segment EBIT margin expanded
250 bps to 7.7%

Operating cash flow strong at ₹6,113.30 Lakh (5.5x PAT)

Full-year EBITDA grew 17.3% YoY to ₹2,766.38 Lakh
Company remains debt-free with nil borrowings

Working capital improved significantly driving CFO turnaround

Risk Factors

Q4 revenue declined 10.2% YoY, continuing sequential weakness

ATM segment (Vortex) Q4 loss widened to ₹172.60 Lakh vs ₹34.42 Lakh YoY

Other income comprised 96% of Q4 PBT - high non-operating dependence

District coverage target likely missed (82% achieved in Q3)

Q4 PAT declined 36.2% QoQ sequentially

Subsidiary drag: subsidiaries contributed 28.3% revenue but 8.7% PAT drag

items.title: STLTECH delivered a strong Q4 FY26 with 37% YoY revenue growth and margin expansion of 480 bps QoQ to 15.1%, driven by the Optical Networking segment....
items.description_html:
STLTECH delivered a strong Q4 FY26 with 37% YoY revenue growth and margin expansion of 480 bps QoQ to 15.1%, driven by the Optical Networking segment. The company turned profitable with PAT of 59 Cr from losses.

The strategic pivot to Enterprise/Data Center exceeded all expectations, with revenue mix reaching 36% vs 30% target, providing a new growth engine. The order book surged to 7,309 Cr, and the company is on track for its FY27 Net Debt/EBITDA target of <1.2x through planned fundraising of up to 2,000 Cr.

Positive Factors

Q4 FY26 revenue grew 37.0% YoY to 1,441 Cr; full year revenue up 18.7% to 4,745 Cr

EBITDA margin expanded 480 bps QoQ to 15.1% in Q4; full year margin up 190 bps to 13.2%

PAT turned positive at 59 Cr in Q4 vs loss of 17 Cr in Q3 and loss of 40 Cr in Q4 FY25

Enterprise/DC revenue mix reached 36.0% of total revenue, exceeding 30% target by 6 percentage points

Order book surged to 7,309 Cr from 5,325 Cr at end Q3, providing strong revenue visibility

Operating cash flow strong at 520 Cr, up 49.4% YoY; CFO/PAT conversion at 9.29x

Net Debt/EBITDA improved to 1.3x (annualized Q4), on track for FY27 target of <1.2x

Risk Factors

Employee costs at 13.9% of revenue for FY26, above management guidance range of 10-12%

Digital and Technology Solutions segment EBITDA dropped to 0 Cr in Q4 from 5 Cr in Q4 FY25

Effective tax rate high at 45.9% in Q4 due to significant deferred tax expense of 45 Cr

Other income at 23 Cr in Q4 includes 30 Cr unrealized forex gains, indicating lower quality income

US litigation with 372 Cr restricted cash; outcome and financial impact not determinable

items.title: Bansal Wire delivered mixed Q4 FY26 results - strong top-line growth of 18.6% YoY and beat on EBITDA per ton (₹6,771 vs ₹6,500 guidance), but missed...
items.description_html:
Bansal Wire delivered mixed Q4 FY26 results - strong top-line growth of 18.6% YoY and beat on EBITDA per ton (₹6,771 vs ₹6,500 guidance), but missed EBITDA growth guidance (16.4% vs 20%) with margin pressure from elevated D&A and finance costs. Cash generation turned strong with positive FCF of ₹1,005 Cr and debt reduction of 9.1%.

Specialty wire business commenced in Q3 and is highly accretive to margins. Near-term catalysts include specialty wire ramp-up and working capital normalization, though execution on FY27 guidance of 20-25% growth will be key.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 18.6% YoY to ₹41,597.86 Cr for FY26

EBITDA per ton of ₹6,771 beat guidance of ₹6,500

Operating cash flow strong at ₹3,330.81 Cr with 2.07x CFO/PAT conversion

Free cash flow turned positive at ₹1,005.51 Cr vs negative in FY25

Total borrowings decreased 9.1% YoY, debt-to-equity improved to 0.39x

Specialty wire business commenced commercial production in Q3

GST regulatory issues resolved, appeals cleared

Subsidiaries contributed 19.4% of consolidated PAT despite only 3.6% of revenue

Risk Factors

EBITDA growth of 16.4% missed guidance of ~20%
Q4 EBITDA margin contracted to 6.73%, lowest in 4 quarters

Depreciation surged 90.7% YoY and finance costs up 59% from capex cycle

Volume declined 3.4% QoQ in Q4 due to natural gas disruption

Capacity utilization at 73.9% vs 90% target

Working capital stretched with receivables outpacing revenue growth

PAT grew only 11.3% YoY vs 18.6% revenue growth

items.description_html:
Race to build global satellite Internet network entering a new phase.
Starlink by far the dominant player at the moment. Operated by SpaceX, the constellation deployed more than 10,300 satellites as of April’26, with regulatory approval for 15,000 & plans to deploy up to 42,000.

Effective April 2027.

items.title: Looks like another big deal is brewing in the consumer brands space. Wishcare, a seven-year-old company known for its hair and skincare products, is l...
items.description_html:
Looks like another big deal is brewing in the consumer brands space.

Wishcare, a seven-year-old company known for its hair and skincare products, is looking for a buyer at a Rs 3,000-3,500 crore valuation.

This represents over a 20x jump from its last valuation of Rs 141 crore in late 2023, when Unilever Ventures led a Rs 20 crore round - it hasn’t raised any other funding since.

In FY24, it's revenues stood at Rs 70 crore.

In FY25, revenues grew 187% to Rs 202 crore, with a net profit of Rs 51 crore - a massive margin.

The company has reached Rs 350 crore in revenue and Rs 100 crore in EBITDA in FY26, according to the report.

items.title: RT Sawyer Merritt: Here's a rough summary of Elon Musk’s 1 hour and 40 minute long testimony today during the OpenAI trial. He will resume his testim...
items.description_html:
RT Sawyer Merritt
Here's a rough summary of Elon Musk’s 1 hour and 40 minute long testimony today during the OpenAI trial. He will resume his testimony tomorrow.

• Argues the case has huge implications: "It is not ok to steal a charity. If the defendants are found not guilty, this case will become caselaw. It’ll give license to looting every charity in America. The consequences of this case go far beyond me or everyone here. The entire foundation of charitable giving in America will be destroyed."

• Says OpenAI was founded as a nonprofit, open-source counterweight to Google, focused on AI safety.

• Claims the shift to a for-profit structure violated that mission.

• Says his AI concerns date back to conversations with Larry Page, who he felt wasn’t taking AI risk seriously.

• Elon says he tried to warn Obama about AI, but that Obama felt AI was not good enough (back then) to seem scary smart. "Here we are in 2026, AI is very smart."

• Believes AI could surpass human intelligence as soon as next year and poses existential risk in the hands of the wrong people: “If you have someone who’s not very trustworthy in charge of AI, that’s very dangerous for the whole world.”

• Elon framed his companies (SpaceX, Neuralink, xAI) as part of a broader mission to protect humanity’s future.

• Emphasized OpenAI’s original goal: AI for the good of humanity, not profit-driven control.

• Elon's main argument is that OpenAI abandoned its founding principles, and that precedent could reshape both AI governance and charitable trust.

• Larry Page refused to speak to Elon Musk again after Elon recruited Ilya Sutskever to join OpenAI. Elon viewed Ilya as the “number one” most valuable member at Google.

• Elon thought in the early days, OpenAI's corporate structure would be a nonprofit funded initially with donations, but there could potentially be a parallel for-profit that is owned by the nonprofit and funds the nonprofit: “We (Sam Altman and Elon) were in agreement that OpenAI would be a 501c3 charity.

• Elon was not opposed to there being a small for-profit that provided funding to the nonprofit, as long as "the tail didn’t wag the dog."

• Elon: “there are very few people who understand venture capital in Silicon Valley like I do."

items.title: Deepinder Goyal on the impact of AI chatbots on Zomato and Blinkit: “The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI ass...
items.description_html:
Deepinder Goyal on the impact of AI chatbots on Zomato and Blinkit:

“The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI assistants are a real but early segment, and currently nowhere close to disrupting our business.”

He adds that, just as Google was not able to disrupt consumer behaviour in travel booking (Expedia, Booking) and shopping (Amazon), general AI chat interfaces will not be able to disrupt “complex, high-frequency” transactions or the “brand associations” tied to specific apps.

“Our moat remains the physical world. AI makes that moat wider by allowing more of India to participate in it.”

More in the letter on how Eternal is using and thinking about AI.

items.title: Growth in large e-commerce platforms in India is increasingly being driven by catering to lower average order values (AOV). Zomato has seen net order ...
items.description_html:
Growth in large e-commerce platforms in India is increasingly being driven by catering to lower average order values (AOV).

Zomato has seen net order value (NOV) growth improve from 13.1% YoY in the June 2025 quarter to 18.8% in the March 2026 quarter.

The key drivers include lowering the free delivery threshold to Rs 99 from Rs 199 and pushing meals priced under Rs 250.

This has increased the number of monthly transacting customers (MTCs) by over 20%, from 20.9 million to 25.4 million over the last nine months.

“Order volume growth is being driven by MTC growth, both from new-to-platform customers and higher frequency among existing budget-conscious customers.”

Deepinder Goyal says that this has decreased AOV, but revenue per order continues to improve.
